title: Warn on contract expressions calling void-return or *T-argument functions
description: At the contract declaration site, walk the expression AST. If any called function has a void return type or takes a mutable pointer argument (*T where T is not const), emit a warning: 'contract expression may have side effects — behavior differs between debug and release builds'. Warning suppressed with a per-contract annotation (syntax TBD). Implementable as an AST pass over requires/ensures/invariant expressions, no new language concepts.
